procedure.title: USGS graduated steel tape water-level measurement excerpt

steps.label: Chalk the lower few feet of the tape

constraints.text: Pull the tape across a piece of blue carpenter's chalk.

steps.label: Review recent measurements from the well, if available, to estimate the hold point on the tape
Lower the weight and tape into the well and record the HOLD value at the measuring point

constraints.text: The weight and tape should be lowered into the water slowly to prevent splashing.

Place the thumb and index finger on the tape graduation that is 0.01 less than the next whole foot mark; continue to lower the end of the tape into the well until the thumb and index finger meet the MP.
Record the graduation value (the HOLD) in the Hold column of the water-level measurement field form.

steps.label: Rapidly bring the tape to the surface and record the wetted CUT length and measurement time

constraints.text: Bring the tape to the surface rapidly before the wetted chalk mark dries and becomes difficult to read.
Record the length of the wetted chalk (the CUT) in the Cut row of the water-level measurement field form.
Record the time of the measurement in the "Time" row of the form.

steps.label: Subtract the CUT from the HOLD and record the depth to water below the MP

constraints.text: Record this number in the "WL below MP" column of the water-level measurement field form. The difference between the HOLD and the CUT is the depth to water below the MP.

steps.label: Apply the tape-calibration correction to the WL below MP value if required

constraints.text: If the tape-calibration procedure indicates that a correction is needed at a given water-level depth or for a given water-level range, apply that correction to the "WL below MP" value by adding or subtracting the appropriate correction.

steps.label: Record the MP correction length and compute the depth to water below land-surface datum
steps.action_object: MP correction and WL below LSD

constraints.text: The MP correction is positive if the MP is above land surface and is negative if the MP is below land surface (see GWPD 3).
Subtract the MP correction from the "WL below MP" value to get the depth to water below or above land-surface datum.
Record the water level in the "WL below LSD" column of the water-level measurement field form.
If the water level is above LSD, record the depth to water in feet below land surface as a negative number.

steps.label: Make a check measurement by repeating steps 1 through 5 with a different HOLD value

constraints.text: The check measurement should be made using a different HOLD value than that used for the original measurement.

quality.review_notes: Second-pass review. Added missing steps: S8 (MP correction and WL below LSD calculation, Instruction 8) and S9 (check measurement, Instruction 9). Original draft conflated Instructions 6-7 into one step and entirely omitted Instructions 8-9. All 9 source instructions now have steps. Constraints expanded to capture exact source text including the thumb/index-finger placement detail (C4), the cut/hold/time form-column targets (C7/C8/C9/C12/C13), and the MP-correction sign convention (C11/C14). Total: 9 steps, 15 constraints (was 6 steps, 4 constraints). S1 (Open the well) and S3 (Review recent measurements, if available) have zero attached constraints by design — both are single self-contained actions with no source-attested guards or parameters; conditional 'if available' is execution context, not a constraint. MIGRATION DROP: original C2 'purpose' type ('A wetted chalk mark will identify that part of the tape that was submerged') was explanatory commentary per the locked taxonomy — explains WHY S2 chalks the tape, does not bind execution.
